authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 43
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary regulation governing contract modifications and revision sequencing in U.S. defense and federal contracting contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 42
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Core contract administration and closeout requirements for fulfillment progression and obligation control.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Part 243
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defense-specific contract-change operations and order/field change handling that materially shape lane behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Defense-only modification assembly and evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines handling, safeguarding, and artifact movement requirements for defense contracting evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-4, Inspection of Supplies and Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines inspection and acceptance evidence standards used before completion and closeout decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative defense lifecycle governance for when ownership and phase transitions move across stakeholders.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 3364 Sector Context
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides official sector classification to ground defense-specific terminology and artifact differentiation.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.91, Product Support Management
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines mission-readiness and support responsibilities that influence completion readiness decisions.

workflow_stages.name: Defense contracting intake and authority check
workflow_stages.description: Capture scope, mission context, authority matrix, and mandatory records before entering execution guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Modification and execution evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Validate change/modification records, acceptance criteria, and closeout evidence before advancing execution state.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness and dependency verification
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate transport/equipment and maintenance dependencies, and map systems-of-truth availability for safe progression.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception routing and boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: When evidence, authority, or policy conflicts arise, emit explicit escalation and adjacent-lane routing artifacts.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Completion and completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce defensible completion outputs only after tenant-specific policy, evidence sufficiency, and owner validation are present.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Risk/blocker register with maintenance and mission notes
systems_of_record: Contract repository or CLIN modification register
Tenant authority and approval workflow systems

allowed: Classify in-scope, blocked, rework, or adjacent-handoff states using evidence-backed checks.
Generate execution updates and completion evidence records when all required defenses and approvals are present.
Escalate ownership when legal, financial, or safety-authority contexts are requested.
escalate: Critical tenant authority, evidence, or systems-of-truth gaps that materially alter disposition.
Public and tenant policy conflicts that alter hold/closeout criteria.
New mission-impact or safety findings appearing after provisional progression.
Unavailable systems required for traceability or approval evidence.
refuse: Requests for legal, licensing, engineering safety, or financial-approval authority outside lane scope.
Completion requests without required acceptance and closeout evidence.
Bypass attempts that skip mandated defense evidentiary gates.

domain_failure_modes: Using generic completion logic without mission-readiness or maintenance constraint checks.
Failing to validate modification and closeout records before transition recommendations.
Over-routing into adjacent legal/financial/engineering authority without handoff.
Ignoring inaccessible systems-of-record when state progression is requested.
Marking closeout complete despite tenant policy changes versus public defaults.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant programs may classify mission-essential, deployment-ready, and maintenance deferral states with different thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat these definitions as tenant-required context and block autonomous completion until confirmed.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some environments use separate contract closeout stores and approval registries with differing precedence.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant authority map for closeout and handoff systems before closure recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Programs may define different red/yellow thresholds for operational blockers.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use tenant policy and return blocked status with explicit escalation if threshold mapping is absent.

notes: Source set combines FAR/DFARS workflow, evidence/control, and NAICS taxonomy with mission-readiness context.
All required workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, and decision boundaries are mapped for explicit lane behavior.
No blocking ambiguity remains because all open points are translated into explicit tenant-required context or escalation conditions.
